title: Phase 38 - Repository Relationship Index
goal: Implement Phase 38 only after project task p37 is completed. Read atlas_projects/atlas-core-evolution/PROJECT.md, EXECUTION_RULES.md, and knowledge/phase38_requirements.md before implementation. Inspect the existing repository grounding service, project scanner, project indexer, relevant-file context service, Git-tracked-file handling, pipeline grounding integration, staging separation, runtime builder, and related tests. Build a deterministic commit-aware repository relationship index, initially focused on Python files. Use verified Git-tracked files only. Index local imports, package relationships, source-to-test and test-to-source relationships, and other deterministic relationships specified in the knowledge document. Persist the index atomically with schema version, Git commit, generation timestamp, file counts, relationship counts, bounded per-file relationships, and indexing failures. Reject or regenerate stale indexes when the Git commit changes. Corrupt indexes must fail safely and be rebuildable. Integrate the relationship index into repository grounding as advisory context expansion while retaining actual tracked file contents as the source of truth. Preserve backward compatibility when the index is disabled, missing, stale, or unavailable. Do not add an external database, vector store, universal language server, or uncontrolled background training process. Include compile checks, AST relationship tests, stale-index tests, untracked-file exclusion, corrupt-index recovery, atomic-write tests, bounded-context tests, grounding integration tests, production-construction tests, and full non-live regression. Commit and push only after validation and manager approval. Restart and verify all services, Discord gateway, runtime heartbeat, clean synchronized Git state, and live production index construction before completing this roadmap task.
